ucs.title: [LLM] Sandworm (Seashell Blizzard / APT44) destructive wiper pre-staging chain — vssadmin + bcdedit + wbadmin + wevtutil
ucs.description: Hunts the signature 'pre-detonation' command sequence Sandworm chains immediately before deploying NotPetya, Olympic Destroyer, Prestige, HermeticWiper, CaddyWiper and IsaacWiper: shadow copies are nuked, the boot recovery policy is disabled, the WBadmin catalog is wiped, and Windows event logs are cleared — usually within a few minutes from the same parent on the same host. This sequence is far more specific to Sandworm/GRU 74455 destructive ops than any single recovery-inhibition command on its own.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmdlines values(Processes.process_name) as tools values(Processes.parent_process_name) as parents values(Processes.parent_process) as parent_cmdlines from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process_name IN ("vssadmin.exe","wmic.exe","wbadmin.exe","bcdedit.exe","wevtutil.exe","powershell.exe","cmd.exe") AND ( (Processes.process_name="vssadmin.exe" AND Processes.process="*delete*shadows*") OR (Processes.process_name="wmic.exe" AND Processes.process="*shadowcopy*delete*") OR (Processes.process_name="wbadmin.exe" AND Processes.process="*delete*catalog*") OR (Processes.process_name="bcdedit.exe" AND (Processes.process="*bootstatuspolicy*ignoreallfailures*" OR Processes.process="*recoveryenabled*no*")) OR (Processes.process_name="wevtutil.exe" AND Processes.process IN ("*cl*system*","*cl*security*","*cl*application*","*clear-log*")) ) by Processes.dest Processes.user _time span=15m | eval distinct_actions=mvappend(if(match(cmdlines,"(?i)shadow"),"shadow_delete",null()), if(match(cmdlines,"(?i)bootstatuspolicy|recoveryenabled"),"bcdedit_disable_recovery",null()), if(match(cmdlines,"(?i)delete\s+catalog"),"wbadmin_purge",null()), if(match(cmdlines,"(?i)wevtutil.*cl|clear-log"),"eventlog_clear",null())) | eval n_actions=mvcount(distinct_actions) | where n_actions >= 3 | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` | `security_content_ctime(lastTime)` | table firstTime lastTime dest user n_actions distinct_actions tools cmdlines parents parent_cmdlines
ucs.kql:
let window = 15m;
let sandworm_actions = DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where FileName in~ ("vssadmin.exe","wmic.exe","wbadmin.exe","bcdedit.exe","wevtutil.exe")
| extend Action = case(
    FileName =~ "vssadmin.exe" and ProcessCommandLine has "delete" and ProcessCommandLine has "shadows", "shadow_delete",
    FileName =~ "wmic.exe" and ProcessCommandLine has "shadowcopy" and ProcessCommandLine has "delete", "shadow_delete",
    FileName =~ "wbadmin.exe" and ProcessCommandLine has "delete" and ProcessCommandLine has "catalog", "wbadmin_purge",
    FileName =~ "bcdedit.exe" and (ProcessCommandLine has "bootstatuspolicy" and ProcessCommandLine has "ignoreallfailures"), "bcdedit_ignore_failures",
    FileName =~ "bcdedit.exe" and (ProcessCommandLine has "recoveryenabled" and ProcessCommandLine has " no"), "bcdedit_disable_recovery",
    FileName =~ "wevtutil.exe" and (ProcessCommandLine has " cl " or ProcessCommandLine has "clear-log"), "eventlog_clear",
    "" )
| where Action != "";
sandworm_actions
| summarize Actions=make_set(Action), Commands=make_set(ProcessCommandLine), Tools=make_set(FileName), Parents=make_set(InitiatingProcessFileName), ParentCmds=make_set(InitiatingProcessCommandLine), FirstSeen=min(Timestamp), LastSeen=max(Timestamp) by DeviceId, DeviceName, AccountName, bin(Timestamp, window)
| extend ActionCount = array_length(Actions), DurationMin = datetime_diff('minute', LastSeen, FirstSeen)
| where ActionCount >= 3
| order by FirstSeen desc
ucs.rationale: Sandworm/Voodoo Bear/APT44 has reused this exact pre-wipe choreography across nearly every destructive payload it has shipped — NotPetya (2017), Olympic Destroyer (2018), Prestige (Oct 2022 Polish/Ukrainian logistics), and the 2022 Ukraine wiper waves (HermeticWiper/CaddyWiper/IsaacWiper). Lone admins occasionally run one of these commands; only a destructive operator runs three or four of them wi

ucs.title: [LLM] Sandworm GPO-pushed wiper deployment (HermeticWiper / CaddyWiper TTP — payload dropped in SYSVOL\Policies + Immediate Scheduled Task)
ucs.description: Hunts Sandworm's signature mass-deployment vector against AD: after compromising a domain controller they drop the wiper binary into SYSVOL\<domain>\Policies\{GUID}\… and modify an existing GPO (typically adding ScheduledTasks.xml / an Immediate Task) so every domain-joined endpoint executes the payload at next gpupdate. Microsoft (MSTIC) and CERT-UA both attributed this exact deployment chain to Seashell Blizzard / GRU 74455 in the February 2022 Ukraine intrusions and re-observed variants through 2023–2024.
ucs.splunk:
| tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Filesystem.file_path) as paths values(Filesystem.file_name) as files values(Filesystem.user) as writer from datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem where Filesystem.file_path="*\\SYSVOL\\*\\Policies\\*" AND (Filesystem.file_name="*.exe" OR Filesystem.file_name="*.dll" OR Filesystem.file_name="*.ps1" OR Filesystem.file_name="*.bat" OR Filesystem.file_name="*.cmd" OR Filesystem.file_name="ScheduledTasks.xml" OR Filesystem.file_name="Registry.pol") by Filesystem.dest Filesystem.file_path Filesystem.file_name Filesystem.user _time span=1h 
| join type=inner Filesystem.dest [
  | tstats summariesonly=true count as remote_exec_hosts dc(Processes.dest) as endpoint_count from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.parent_process_name IN ("svchost.exe","taskeng.exe","taskhostw.exe") AND (Processes.process="*\\SYSVOL\\*\\Policies\\*" OR Processes.parent_process="*gpupdate*" OR Processes.process_name="gpupdate.exe") by Processes.dest Processes.process Processes.parent_process _time span=2h | rename Processes.dest as dest 
] 
| where endpoint_count >= 5 
| `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` | table firstTime dest writer paths files endpoint_count remote_exec_hosts
ucs.kql:
let sysvolDrops = DeviceFileEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where FolderPath has @"\SYSVOL\" and FolderPath has @"\Policies\"
| where ActionType in ("FileCreated","FileRenamed","FileModified")
| where (FileName endswith ".exe" or FileName endswith ".dll" or FileName endswith ".ps1" 
     or FileName endswith ".bat" or FileName endswith ".cmd" or FileName endswith ".vbs"
     or FileName =~ "ScheduledTasks.xml" or FileName =~ "Registry.pol")
| project DropTime=Timestamp, DC=DeviceName, DCId=DeviceId, DroppedFile=FileName, DroppedPath=FolderPath, DropActor=InitiatingProcessAccountName, DropProc=InitiatingProcessFileName, DropCmd=InitiatingProcessCommandLine;
let domainExec = DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ ("svchost.exe","taskeng.exe","taskhostw.exe","gpupdate.exe")
     or InitiatingProcessCommandLine has "gpupdate"
     or FolderPath has @"\SYSVOL\" 
     or ProcessCommandLine has @"\SYSVOL\"
| project ExecTime=Timestamp, ExecHost=DeviceName, ExecHostId=DeviceId, ExecFile=FileName, ExecCmd=ProcessCommandLine, ExecParent=InitiatingProcessFileName;
sysvolDrops
| join kind=inner (domainExec) on $left.DroppedFile == $right.ExecFile
| where ExecTime between (DropTime .. DropTime + 6h)
| summarize FirstDrop=min(DropTime), FirstExec=min(ExecTime), DroppedFiles=make_set(DroppedFile), DropPaths=make_set(DroppedPath), DropActors=make_set(DropActor), AffectedHosts=dcount(ExecHostId), HostList=make_set(ExecHost,50), SampleCmds=make_set(ExecCmd,10) by DC
| where AffectedHosts >= 5
| order by FirstDrop desc
ucs.rationale: Sandworm specifically pushes destructive payloads through GPO rather than EDR-noisy tools like PsExec because (a) it propagates to the entire domain instantly via gpupdate and (b) it inherits SYSTEM execution on every endpoint without needing per-host auth — exactly the technique MSTIC documented for HermeticWiper (Feb 2022) and CERT-UA documented for CaddyWiper. Legitimate admins almost never wri
